architecture_style: Integrated resort; three towers with sky park
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Resort complex with museum, mall, convention centre, and cantilevered SkyPark pool deck visible from bay viewpoints.
facts: Spectra light show plays in the event plaza below the towers.
SkyPark observation tickets are timed at peak sunset hours.
history: Moshe Safdie's design became Singapore's post-2010 skyline signature across from the financial district.
significance: Anchor of the Marina Bay leisure cluster with Gardens by the Bay.

license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Merlion fountain facing Marina Bay—tourist icon referencing Singapore's fishing-village past and 'lion city' name.
facts: Night crowds compete for tripod space during Marina laser shows.
Smaller merlion statues exist elsewhere on the island.
history: Designed by Fraser Brunner as a Singapore Tourism Board symbol; the current site improves harbour sightlines.
significance: Mandatory photo stop framing the bay skyline.

license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Supertree grove light shows, Cloud Forest dome, and Flower Dome seasonal displays on reclaimed waterfront land.
facts: OCBC Skyway tickets sell out before garden rhapsody times.
Cooled conservatories offer relief from equatorial humidity.
history: Part of Marina Bay's land reclamation strategy to add green tourism capacity beside the financial core.

architecture_style: Chinese moral tale dioramas in outdoor park
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Surreal sculpture park illustrating Chinese folklore and morality tales—ten courts of hell section is graphic but famous.
facts: Free admission makes it a budget-friendly counterpoint to Marina attractions.
Mosquito repellent helps on humid afternoons under trees.
history: Aw family built Tiger Balm fortune into this educational garden; state took over management in later decades.
significance: Weird heritage slice rarely duplicated elsewhere in Southeast Asia.

architecture_style: Colourful shed roofs over dining strip
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Riverfront dining and nightlife in restored godowns, linked by pedestrian bridges to Boat Quay upstream.
facts: River taxis connect to Marina Bay Sands without road traffic.
Bungee-style swings operate from a tall riverside rig for thrill seekers.
history: Named for Governor Clarke; warehouses turned into clubs after clean-up of the Singapore River campaign.
significance: Evening temperature drop makes this a social heat sink for visitors.

description: Riverfront galleries on trade, faith, and design linking Singapore to wider Asian regions.
facts: Friday evenings often extend hours with performances.
Boat quay bars sit one block south along the walk.
history: Government offices became a thematic museum as the civic district pedestrianised.
significance: Curatorial anchor for multicultural origin stories.
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.

description: Rooftop orchid garden and Buddhist culture museum below prayer halls with processions on feast days.
facts: Modest dress enforced for upper relic chamber.
Maxwell Food Centre sits behind for hawker contrast.
history: Built to enshrine a tooth relic tradition brought from Myanmar networks.
significance: Chinatown's monumental modern temple counterweight to shophouse density.
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.

description: National orchid garden hybrids and lakeside concert lawns on the MRT Circle Line stop.
facts: Early hours beat equatorial humidity in the rainforest valley.
Symphony stage hosts weekend classical picnics.
history: Rubber experiments here spread across colonial plantations; later eras emphasised public leisure.
significance: UNESCO site rare for a tropical botanic institution.
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.

description: Souvenir arcades and hawker stalls under lanterns between temple gates and boutique hotels.
facts: Mid-Autumn festival hangs dense lantern canopies.
Heritage centre explains migration waves in air-conditioned galleries.
history: Conservation gating balanced tourism with resident temple life.
significance: Densest pedestrian retail in historic Chinatown.
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.

description: Bumboats and lit façades linking Clarke Quay nightlife to civic district bridges.
facts: River taxis beat road detours during F1 road closures.
Cavenagh Bridge marks the civic end of the walk.
history: Clean-up campaigns removed polluting trades before glass towers framed both banks.
significance: Cooling breezes for evening dining after Marina heat.
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.

name_en: Gardens by the Bay Supertree Grove
architecture_style: Steel vertical gardens with canopy walk
description: Evening light shows on tree-like towers with OCBC Skyway tickets above succulents.
facts: Cloud Forest dome sits west for cooled mist walks.
Marina Barrage kite flyers appear on breezy Sundays upstream.
history: National gardens strategy reclaimed land into horticultural tourism capacity.
significance: Night skyline choreography facing the financial towers.
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.

year_built: 2015 union of City Hall and former Supreme Court
architecture_style: Neoclassical civic buildings linked by bridge
description: Southeast Asian modern art across linked courtyards and rooftop sculpture fields.
facts: Colonial courtrooms host rotating installations.
Smokehouse cafés sit under the glass link bridges.
history: Two national monuments merged into one climate-controlled museum circuit.
significance: Largest public display of Singapore and regional modernism.
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
